releaseBody:
(AI Generated).

## Freed 26.5.3114-dev

This dev build adds Facebook zero-post diagnostics and keeps the RSS retry and dev-channel fixes ready for the next soak

### Fixes

- Facebook sync now reports why extracted posts normalize to zero usable items.
- Zero usable Facebook scrape results still surface as sync issues instead of healthy success.
- RSS scheduled polling backs off after runtime deferrals instead of retrying every 15 seconds.
- Broken RSS feeds record retry windows after failed fetches.
- Installed dev builds continue using the dev release channel after reinstall.
- Social sync controls recover cleanly after memory-pressure deferrals.
- Adaptive memory guardrails give long-running provider sync more headroom on high-memory systems.
- Capture and scraper reliability fixes.
- Release build and type-safety cleanup.
- Reader, feed, and header polish.
- Aligns the social memory preflight e2e with transient provider-health memory pressure
- Keeps the Phase 7 docs and public roadmap aligned with the new social scrape memory behavior

### Follow-ups

- Run the next Facebook sync soak on this build and inspect the normalization summary if the provider still returns zero usable posts.
- Keep reducing desktop sync memory pressure if runtime health still shows renderer stalls.
- Install this build and verify RSS failures stop piling up in sync health
- Run the next Facebook sync soak after explicit provider-risk confirmation, using the new diagnostics if results are still zero

### Downloads

**macOS:** `.dmg` (Apple Silicon or Intel, signed + notarized)  
**Windows:** `.exe` (NSIS installer)  
**Linux:** `.AppImage`  

> macOS downloads are signed and notarized for normal Gatekeeper installation.
